title: How Confidence Level Affects CI Width

steps.observe: Note the **95% CI** bounds in the results panel. How wide is the interval?
steps.reveal: The 95% CI is roughly (9.7, 11.1) — a width of about 1.4 years.

steps.instruction: Change the confidence level dropdown to **90%**.
steps.observe: How did the interval change? Is it wider or narrower than the 95% CI?
steps.reveal: The 90% CI is narrower. We're accepting a higher chance of missing the true parameter (10% instead of 5%), so we don't need as wide a net.

steps.instruction: Now change the confidence level to **99%**.
steps.observe: Compare all three intervals. Which is widest? Which is narrowest?
steps.reveal: 99% is widest, 90% is narrowest. There's always a trade-off: **higher confidence = wider interval = less precision**. You can be very confident or very precise, but not both — unless you increase the sample size.
